claims.claim_text: Anthropic positions the Claude Agent SDK as programmatic access to the same core runtime foundations that power Claude Code.
claims.rationale: Anthropic's announcement says the Claude Agent SDK gives access to the same core tools, context-management systems, and permissions frameworks that power Claude Code.

claims.claim_text: Anthropic's public guidance describes agent work as a gather-context, take-action, verify-work, repeat loop.
claims.span_hint: section: 2. The Gather, Act, Verify Loop
claims.rationale: The primary Anthropic article explicitly presents this loop, but the canonical page resolves to claude.com, which is outside the current allowlist.

claims.claim_text: The SDK exposes runtime features such as tools, context management, permissions, subagents, and hooks.
claims.span_hint: section: 3. What the Claude Agent SDK Adds
claims.rationale: Anthropic's product announcement names core tools, context management, permissions, subagents, and hooks as Agent SDK capabilities.

claims.claim_text: The current SDK documentation describes capabilities including reading files, editing code, running commands, web operations, MCP integration, and session/context features.
claims.span_hint: section: 1. From Chat Integration to Agent Runtime
claims.rationale: The detailed capability list is documented in the official SDK docs, but those docs currently resolve to code.claude.com, which is off-allowlist.

claims.claim_text: The minimal Python example is runnable with the `claude-agent-sdk` package installed and an `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` configured.
claims.span_hint: section: 3. What the Claude Agent SDK Adds | paragraph after first Python example
claims.rationale: The Python SDK reference documents installation and environment-key setup, but the canonical doc host is not on the allowlist.

claims.claim_text: Built-in tools cover local work such as file reads and edits, shell commands, content search, and web operations.
claims.span_hint: section: 4. Tool Boundaries: Built-In Tools, MCP, and Custom Code
claims.rationale: The official SDK and hooks docs enumerate these tool families, but the documentation host is off-allowlist.

claims.claim_text: The SDK hook system can intercept tool-use and session lifecycle events, including pre/post tool hooks and session start/stop.
claims.span_hint: section: 5. Permissions, Hooks, and Approval Boundaries
claims.rationale: The hooks reference documents these event types directly, but the canonical documentation host is outside the current allowlist.

claims.claim_text: MCP servers extend the SDK with structured external tools and can be configured inside the agent runtime.
claims.span_hint: section: 4. Tool Boundaries: Built-In Tools, MCP, and Custom Code | paragraph before second Python example
claims.rationale: The SDK MCP documentation is the primary source for this capability, but it resolves to code.claude.com rather than an allowlisted host.

claims.claim_text: Subagents can isolate context by operating in their own context window separate from the main conversation.
claims.span_hint: section: 6. Sessions, Context Compaction, and Drift
claims.rationale: Anthropic's subagents documentation states this explicitly, but the page is hosted on a non-allowlisted domain.

claims.claim_text: The Claude Agent SDK is the renamed successor to the Claude Code SDK for broader agent use beyond coding workflows.
claims.rationale: Anthropic's primary rename explanation appears in the Claude Agent SDK blog post, which currently resolves to claude.com and therefore needs allowlist expansion.

claims.claim_text: The SDK supports both Python and TypeScript.
claims.rationale: The primary SDK references for Python and TypeScript are official but hosted on code.claude.com, which is not currently allowlisted.

further_reading.title: Enabling Claude Code to Work More Autonomously
further_reading.why_relevant: Best allowlisted overview of the Agent SDK's relationship to Claude Code, plus hooks and subagents.

further_reading.title: New Capabilities for Building Agents on the Anthropic API
further_reading.why_relevant: Good adjacent context on Anthropic's broader agent-building primitives, including MCP connectivity and longer-lived context.

further_reading.title: Claude Agents
further_reading.why_relevant: Useful high-level product framing for how Anthropic positions agents, Claude Code, and agent workflows.

notes: Most detailed SDK/runtime claims in this module map to official Anthropic docs that now resolve to code.claude.com or claude.com rather than allowlisted anthropic.com hosts. Expect allowlist-expansion decisions for capability-level citations (tools, hooks, MCP, subagents, Python/TypeScript refs, and rename rationale).
